error.signature: Jira REST API pagination returns incomplete results or different behavior between v2 and v3

verdict.summary: Jira REST API v2 and v3 have different pagination behaviors. v2 returns 'total' which can change between pages (as issues are created/modified). v3 sometimes omits 'total'. maxResults is capped server-side (usually 50-100) regardless of requested value.

dead_ends.action: Set maxResults=1000 to get all issues in one request
dead_ends.why_fails: Jira server silently caps maxResults to 50-100 (configurable by admin). You get at most 100 results regardless of what you request.

dead_ends.action: Use 'total' field to determine when pagination is complete
dead_ends.why_fails: In Jira v3, 'total' may be absent. In v2, 'total' can change between page requests as issues are created/modified mid-pagination.

workarounds.action: Paginate until returned results count < maxResults (not based on 'total')
workarounds.how: while True: results = jira_search(startAt=offset, maxResults=50); if len(results) < 50: break; offset += 50

workarounds.action: Use JQL with ORDER BY and createdDate filter for stable pagination
workarounds.how: JQL: 'project = X ORDER BY created ASC' with startAt pagination. Ordering ensures consistent page contents.

workarounds.action: Use Jira's scroll API (experimental) for large result sets
workarounds.how: For Jira Cloud: use the /search endpoint with 'expand' and pagination tokens instead of offset-based pagination
